name: Money Mart Check Cashing Money Transfers Milwaukee
description_short: Money Mart offers check cashing, money transfers, bill payment, and mobile top-ups at their Milwaukee location in partnership with Piggly Wiggly.
description_long:
Money Mart is a financial services provider operating in Milwaukee, Wisconsin, with a location at 123 West Oklahoma Ave in partnership with Piggly Wiggly. The company positions itself as a community-focused alternative financial services provider serving residents who may lack traditional banking access or prefer specialized money services.

The company offers a diverse range of services including check cashing without requiring a bank account, domestic and international money transfers to home countries through multiple payout channels (banks, shops, pharmacies), mobile phone top-ups across the United States, Mexico, and Central America, and bill payment services covering utilities such as electricity, gas, water, phone, cell, and cable. These services are designed to provide one-stop convenience for consumers managing multiple financial transactions.

Money Mart distinguishes itself through its emphasis on compliance and ethical business practices, explicitly stating "The Money Mart Promise" focused on regulatory adherence and workplace ethics. The company highlights partnerships (such as with Piggly Wiggly) to expand accessibility and emphasizes core values of collaboration, diversity, integrity, and relationships. Their marketing specifically targets underbanked populations by advertising check cashing without bank account requirements and offering money transfer options to home countries, suggesting a focus on immigrant and international communities.

The company operates as a straightforward alternative financial services provider with no claims of credit building, financial counseling, or loan products. The website lacks detailed information about fees, exchange rates for transfers, or specific APRs, making it difficult to assess true cost competitiveness. Limited digital presence and a single known location suggest a smaller regional operation rather than a national chain.
address: 1322 S César E. Chávez Dr, Milwaukee, WI 53204

company_info.headquarters: 1322 S César E. Chávez Dr, Milwaukee, WI 53204

pros: Check cashing available without requiring a bank account, reducing barriers for unbanked consumers
Piggly Wiggly partnership provides convenient retail location for accessing services
Multiple money transfer payout options (banks, shops, pharmacies) offer flexibility for international recipients
One-stop service location for check cashing, bill payment, money transfers, and phone top-ups
Serves international communities with phone top-ups across US, Mexico, and Central America
Explicit commitment to compliance and ethical business practices documented on website
Covers comprehensive utility bill payments in single location (electricity, gas, water, phone, cable)
cons: Website provides no fee schedule, making it impossible to compare check cashing or transfer costs against competitors
Single known location in Milwaukee limits accessibility for broader geographic market
No exchange rate information provided for international money transfers
Limited digital services or online capability mentioned; appears to be cash/in-person only
No customer reviews, ratings, or third-party verification of service quality available on website
best_for: Unbanked and underbanked residents of Milwaukee without traditional bank accounts
Immigrants and international workers needing to send money to home countries in Latin America and beyond
Consumers seeking convenient one-stop access to check cashing, bill payment, and money transfer services
Mobile phone users in the US, Mexico, and Central America needing affordable top-up options
diagnosis: Money Mart is best for Milwaukee residents without bank accounts who need accessible check cashing, bill payment, and especially immigrants requiring international money transfers to Latin America. The main caveat is the complete absence of published fees and exchange rates on their website, making it impossible to evaluate whether their services are competitively priced—consumers should call ahead or visit in person to compare costs with competitors.
services: Check cashing without bank account requirement
Multiple money transfer payout methods (banks, retail shops, pharmacies)
Mobile phone top-ups for US networks
International mobile top-ups (Mexico and Central America)
Phone and cell phone bill payment
